name: Pico Catoute and Pico de la Cernella – circular via the Laguna de la Rebeza from Colinas del Campo de Martín Moro
name_original: Pico Catoute y Pico de la Cernella – circular por la Laguna de la Rebeza desde Colinas del Campo de Martín Moro
region_intro: Hiking trails around Igüeña traverse a mountainous landscape in the El Bierzo Alto region of León, Spain. The area features diverse natural environments, including varied forests of pines, oaks, hollies, and yews. Rivers like the Boeza create natural pools and picturesque spots, while the terrain offers significant elevation changes. This region provides a range of hiking experiences from moderate paths to challenging treks. Best hiking trails around Igüeña The most popular hiking route is Pico Catoute and Pico de la Cernella – circular via the Laguna de la Rebeza from Colinas del Campo de Martín Moro, a difficult 8.9 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 6 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain to two prominent peaks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna de la Rebeza y Campo de Santiago – circular desde Colinas de Campo (Reserva de la Biosfera Valles de Omaña y Luna), a difficult 10.8 miles (17.4 km) path. This circular route explores a high-altitude lagoon and the Campo de Santiago within a biosphere reserve. Local hikers also love the Campo de Santiago y Ermita de Santiago – return trip from Fasgar via the Camino Olividado (Biosphere Reserve Valles de Omaña y Luna), a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through forests and historical sites, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Igüeña is defined by mountainous terrain, varied forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Igüeña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Igüeña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Igüeña?
A: Igüeña offers a diverse mountainous landscape within the El Bierzo Alto region. You'll encounter varied forests, including pines, oaks, hollies, and yews. The terrain features significant elevation changes, and rivers like the Boeza create natural pools and picturesque spots.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in Igüeña?
A: Yes, Igüeña offers routes for various ability levels. Among the 15 hiking trails available, 3 are classified as easy, providing accessible options for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed stroll.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Igüeña?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Igüeña are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, consider the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041030422/pico-catoute-and-pico-de-la-cernella-circular-via-the-laguna-de-la-rebeza-from-colinas-del-campo-de-martin-moro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Catoute and Pico de la Cernella – circular via the Laguna de la Rebeza from Colinas del Campo de Martín Moro&lt;/a&gt;, or the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1276601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Gualta Viewpoint – Gualta Waterfall loop from Noceda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to discover on the hiking trails near Igüeña?
A: Yes, the region boasts beautiful water features. You can find waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gualta Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1276601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Gualta Viewpoint – Gualta Waterfall loop from Noceda&lt;/a&gt; route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Paseo de los Molinos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see while hiking in Igüeña?
A: The area is rich in history. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Campo de Martín Moro Toledano&lt;/a&gt;, situated in a sprawling meadow. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041151595/campo-de-santiago-y-ermita-de-santiago-return-trip-from-fasgar-via-the-camino-olividado-biosphere-reserve-valles-de-omana-y-luna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Santiago y Ermita de Santiago – return trip from Fasgar via the Camino Olividado&lt;/a&gt; route also takes you past ancient pilgrim hospitals.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Igüeña?
A: Experienced hikers will find plenty of challenging options. Out of 15 routes, 8 are rated as difficult, often involving significant elevation gain. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041157181/laguna-de-la-rebeza-y-campo-de-santiago-circular-desde-colinas-de-campo-reserva-de-la-biosfera-valles-de-omana-y-luna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Rebeza y Campo de Santiago – circular desde Colinas de Campo&lt;/a&gt;, which explores a high-altitude lagoon and a biosphere reserve.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Igüeña?
A: The hiking trails in Igüeña are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.9 out of 5 stars from over 25 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, stunning mountain views, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any prominent mountain peaks accessible via hiking trails in Igüeña?
A: Yes, the region is known for its mountainous terrain and offers access to several peaks. You can hike to summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Nevadín (2,077 m)&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Catoute (2,117 m)&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Cernella (2,119 m)&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views.

Q: What is the best way to experience the 'Camino Olvidado' near Igüeña?
A: Several stages of the historical 'Camino Olvidado' (Forgotten Way) pass through or near Igüeña. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041151595/campo-de-santiago-y-ermita-de-santiago-return-trip-from-fasgar-via-the-camino-olividado-biosphere-reserve-valles-de-omana-y-luna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Santiago y Ermita de Santiago – return trip from Fasgar via the Camino Olividado&lt;/a&gt;, which guides you through forests and past historical sites.

Q: Are there any interesting man-made structures or historical sites along the trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, you can explore intriguing historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1657319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgoso Dam&lt;/a&gt;, a former hydroelectric power station from the 1930s, is a notable point of interest along some routes, particularly those associated with the 'Ruta de los Molinos'.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in total around Igüeña?
A: There are 15 distinct hiking routes available around Igüeña, offering a wide range of experiences from easy walks to difficult mountain ascents.
tips: From the top of Pico Catoute, located 2,112 meters above sea level, we will have sublime panoramic views next to the geodesic post and the mailbox.
Located at an altitude of 2,119 meters, the Pico de la Cernella offers us a beautiful panoramic view of the surrounding area and the Rebeza Lagoon at our feet.
Located in a beautiful enclave and surrounded by mountains, Laguna de la Rebeza is an ideal place to take a break and recover energy to continue with our route.
